dublinCore.dc:title: 119 SRES 565 IS: Recognizing that facilities that produce renewable electricity are the cheapest power-generating facilities to operate and reliance on fossil fuel-generating facilities to meet growing power demand drives up wholesale electricity prices.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Whitehouse (for himself, Mr. Merkley, Mr. Schatz, Mr. Markey, Mr. Van Hollen, Ms. Duckworth, Mr. Padilla, Mr. Welch, and Ms. Blunt Rochester) submitted the following resolution; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources

form.official-title: Recognizing that facilities that produce renewable electricity are the cheapest power-generating facilities to operate and reliance on fossil fuel-generating facilities to meet growing power demand drives up wholesale electricity prices.

whereas.text: Whereas electricity prices are generally determined by the level of demand for power and the cost of generating such power used to meet the demand;
Whereas the demand for power in the United States is growing faster than at any point in the past 2 decades;
Whereas power generators with the lowest operating costs are typically dispatched first, thereby meeting the demand for power at the lowest cost;
Whereas, as the demand for power increases, power generators with higher operating costs are dispatched, thereby driving up wholesale electricity prices;
Whereas fossil-power generation from coal, fossil gas, and oil has high operating costs due to the cost of fuel and maintenance; and
Whereas the generation of wind, solar, and other renewable resources has near-zero operating costs: Now, therefore, be it

paragraph.text: facilities that produce renewable electricity are the cheapest electricity-generating facilities to operate to meet the demand for power; and
reliance on fossil fuel-generating facilities to meet the growing demand for power drives up wholesale electricity prices.
